assertions.text: I was traveling at highway speed, about 90 km/h.
I struck the back of a parked vehicle with my front bumper.
I hit the brakes only at the last moment.
There was a single impact (struck the back of a parked vehicle).
Braking occurred after most of the approach and immediately before the impact (braked only at the last moment, then struck).
The collision involved the front bumper impacting the rear of another vehicle (implying a front-to-rear collision; no claim about high severity beyond that).

verdicts.evidence: The claimant said [I was traveling at highway speed, about 90 km/h.](T1) but the recorded vehicle speed shows a first pre-crash value of [34.0](T2) km/h and a last pre-crash value of [32.0](T2) km/h, with a max of [36.0](T2) km/h — far below 90 km/h.

The claimant said [I struck the back of a parked vehicle with my front bumper.](T1) and the crash pulse shows a negative longitudinal delta-V (cumulative) with a max of [‑18.0](T1) km/h at [182.5](T1) ms indicating the vehicle decelerated on impact consistent with a frontal impact into another vehicle; lateral delta-V is small ([‑1.0](T1) km/h) suggesting primarily front-to-rear contact.

The claimant said [I hit the brakes only at the last moment.](T1) and the brake channel shows a transition from off to on at [‑0.5](T2) s before impact, with fraction on [0.18](T2) indicating braking began very shortly before the collision.

The claimant said [There was a single impact (struck the back of a parked vehicle).](T1) and the EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and a single crash pulse with final longitudinal delta-V [‑18.0](T2) km/h, consistent with one impact.

The claimant said [Braking occurred after most of the approach and immediately before the impact (braked only at the last moment, then struck).](T1) — speed was nearly constant from [34.0](T2) to [32.0](T2) km/h in the 5 s before impact while the brake transition occurred at [‑0.5](T2) s before impact, supporting that braking happened immediately before the impact.

The claimant said [The collision involved the front bumper impacting the rear of another vehicle (implying a front-to-rear collision; no claim about high severity beyond that).](T1) — while the negative longitudinal delta-V of [‑18.0](T2) km/h indicates a frontal deceleration, axis alignment between device axes and vehicle frame is not documented, so labeling it specifically as 'front bumper impacting the rear' is an inference beyond sensor-provided frame alignment and is unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single frontal-type impact with a longitudinal delta-V of [‑18.0](T2) km/h, pre-crash speed around [32–36](T2) km/h, and braking applied [‑0.5](T2) s before impact; these data contradict the 90 km/h claim but support the timing, single-impact, and frontal-contact assertions, while the exact bumper-to-bumper geometry is unverifiable from device-frame axes.

citations.claim: I was traveling at highway speed, about 90 km/h.
I struck the back of a parked vehicle with my front bumper.
I hit the brakes only at the last moment.
There was a single impact (struck the back of a parked vehicle).
Braking occurred after most of the approach and immediately before the impact (braked only at the last moment, then struck).
The collision involved the front bumper impacting the rear of another vehicle (implying a front-to-rear collision; no claim about high severity beyond that).

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
